explanation:
Mathematical skills provide universal tools—algebra, geometry, calculus, and statistics are essential across engineering, science, finance, and everyday life.

## How It's Calculated

This calculator applies the percentage formula to your input values, checking typical ranges and alerting you to unusual or unreasonable values.

## Final Notes

These calculations are for reference purposes. For critical decisions (financial, medical, legal), please consult with a qualified professional. Values are current as of 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.q: How do I calculate a percentage increase?
faq.a: Formula: New = N·(1 + x/100). Multiply the original value by 1 plus the percentage increase.

faq.q: How do I calculate a percentage discount?
faq.a: Formula: New = N·(1 - x/100). Multiply the original value by 1 minus the discount percentage.

faq.q: How do I calculate percentage change?
faq.a: Formula: ((B - A) / A) × 100. This shows the relative change from value A to value B.

faq.q: How do I calculate VAT or sales tax?
faq.a: Formula: Final Price = Price · (1 + VAT/100). Add the tax percentage to the original price.

faq.q: What's the difference between percentage and percentage point?
faq.a: A percentage point is the absolute difference between two percentages. For example, 10% to 15% is a 5 percentage point increase, but a 50% relative increase.

faq.q: Is this calculator free to use?
faq.a: Yes, all our calculators are completely free with no signup or registration required.
Results are for reference purposes. We use internationally validated standard formulas. For critical decisions, consult a professional.

faq.q: Do you save my calculation data?
faq.a: No. All calculations happen in your browser. We don't send any data to our servers.
